title: fluffy yeasty algerian cabbage tagine
description: a quick-fix algerian dish featuring hispi sweetheart cabbage, perfect for a weeknight dinner

ingredients.name: instant dry yeast
ingredients.notes: rapid rise yeast for a quicker fermentation time

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: kosher salt for a milder flavor

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: freshly ground for a more intense flavor

ingredients.name: grated romano cheese
ingredients.notes: for an extra burst of flavor

instructions: in a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, instant dry yeast, and salt. whisk until well combined.
add the grated romano cheese and mix until just combined.
in a separate bowl, whisk together the olive oil and water. add the mixture to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
knead the dough for 5 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
preheat a tagine or large Dutch oven over binchotan charcoal to 400f (200c).
punch down the dough and divide it into 6-8 equal pieces. shape each piece into a ball and flatten slightly into a disk.
place the cabbage disks in the preheated tagine and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until tender and slightly caramelized.
in a small bowl, mix the binchotan charcoal powder with the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil. brush the mixture evenly over the cabbage disks.
return the tagine to the oven and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the cabbage is tender and the tops are slightly charred.
remove from heat and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired
